q: Is Selah a replacement for a therapist or pastor?
a: No. Selah is a companion — for the questions you think of at 11pm, the moments between sessions, the days you need scripture and wisdom in your pocket. For ongoing care, keep your therapist, pastor, and GP.

q: How does Selah decide what's biblical?
a: Selah was formed from a curated library — theologians, biblical commentators, pastors, psychologists, psychiatrists, philosophers, and therapists who share a historic biblical worldview. Their books, lectures, and talks are the corpus Selah draws from. Scripture is always the first source; the trusted teachers are how it's read.
Yes. Conversations are encrypted, never sold, and never used to train models outside Selah. You can delete your history anytime.

q: What about crises — suicide, abuse, psychosis?
a: Selah always hands you to a human in crisis: 988 in the US, 13 11 14 in Australia, and local equivalents. It will never try to be the only help you have.
Selah was built by the team at Futures — pastors, clinicians, and engineers who got tired of people being handed generic AI when they were in pain.

q: What does founding membership actually get me?
a: Lifetime access at the founding price, early features, a seat in the monthly Q&A with Ashley and the team, and first dibs on Selah Circles when they launch.
